rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NADINE D. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/17/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No bank statements have been filed. The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). For the first account in conservatorships fiduciaries must also file original account statements showing the account balance on the date immediately preceding the date the fiduciary was appointed (Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2) and LRSF 14.34.D.1). 2) Paragraph 22 of the instant petition asserts that no compensation was paid to petitioner or his attorneys, but Schedule C of the post-death accounting shows two payments to ARCR for "attorney expenses." What were these expenses and why were they paid without court authorization? 3) Small estate affidavits pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100 must be conventionally filed (LRSF 14.59.C.2) and each affidavit must have a certified copy of the decedent's death certificate attached (Probate Code Section 13101(d)). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 03/03/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERTRUDE BEVERLY WELLS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/10/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Charles Schwab did not receive notice of the petition. In a further supplement, please clarify if Charles Schwab consents to the requested distribution to the beneficiaries. 2) Do the beneficiaries agree to receive the funds from Schwab as inherited IRA accounts? If so, please file consents from each beneficiary. Please provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 03/03/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
